content:
Case 20:
Initial presentation:
- Age: 77
- Sex: male
- Chief complaint: Progressive dyspnea, ascites, and a painful gluteal mass in a patient with CLL
- Vital signs: BP 105/65 mmHg, HR 112/min, RR 22/min, Temp 37.7 °C, SpO₂ 92%
Setting: Acute hematology intake
Capabilities: Rapid bedside assessment with point-of-care imaging and routine laboratory panels is available, but invasive sampling or specialized molecular diagnostics are not yet ready.
Provide either a request for a single information item or solve.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "physical examination"}}

History Physical:
2025-09-23: Presents with progressive dyspnea, abdominal distension, and painful swelling of the left gluteal region with induration over old scars from animal bites. Left leg edema present. Pulmonary exam: diminished breath sounds with basal crackles. Vital signs as listed. Current meds: beclometasone/formoterol inhaler, HCTZ, levothyroxine, vitamins, pantoprazole, amlodipine, aciclovir, allopurinol, bisoprolol, candesartan, atorvastatin, zanubrutinib 160 mg daily.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "complete blood count with differential"}}

Baseline Labs:
Hb 9.2 g/dL, WBC 41 ×10⁹/L (82% lymphocytes), Plt 78 ×10⁹/L. Coagulation: aPTT 31 s, INR 1.1, fibrinogen 281 mg/dL, D-dimer 2987 µg/L FEU. Chemistry: Na 138 mmol/L, K 4.4 mmol/L, creatinine 1.4 mg/dL, AST 185 U/L, ALT 210 U/L, bilirubin 2.8 mg/dL, LDH 852 U/L, uric acid 7.8 mg/dL, albumin 2.8 g/dL, total protein 5.2 g/dL, NT-proBNP 780 pg/mL, Troponin T 0.02 ng/mL, IgG 359 mg/dL.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "CT scan of chest, abdomen, and pelvis"}}

response.request: CT scan of chest, abdomen, and pelvis

content:
'extended_evaluations' available in round 2.
CT/PET and other advanced imaging are listed under extended evaluations, which are not available in round 1. Consider requesting initial imaging (e.g., chest X‑ray/ultrasound) or baseline labs in this round instead.
(4 invalid requests remaining before case is cancelled. Consider solving with available information or requesting a different item.)
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "abdominal ultrasound"}}

Initial Imaging:
Chest X-ray: bilateral pleural effusions with basal atelectasis (right > left); Abdominal ultrasound: four-quadrant ascites, multiple enlarged paraaortic and mesenteric nodes up to 6.5 cm, bulky hepatic hilum conglomerate, hepatomegaly with nodular contour, splenomegaly 18.2 × 9.6 cm. Echocardiogram: LVEF 51% with RV dilatation and pulmonary hypertension.

{"response": {"action": "solve", "solve": {"working_diagnosis": "Richter Transformation of CLL to Diffuse Large B-Cell Lymphoma (DLBCL), with bulky nodal disease, hepatosplenic involvement, ascites, pleural effusions, and extranodal gluteal mass (at prior animal-bite scar site), on BTK inhibitor background", "differentials": ["CLL progression without transformation (accelerated CLL)", "De novo DLBCL with concurrent CLL", "Soft tissue infection / necrotizing fasciitis at old animal-bite scar site (immunocompromised host on zanubrutinib)", "Primary effusion lymphoma or plasmablastic lymphoma", "Burkitt lymphoma transformation"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "Urgent core biopsy of the gluteal mass (and/or a readily accessible enlarged node) for histopathology, immunohistochemistry (CD20, CD10, BCL6, MYC, BCL2, Ki-67), FISH (MYC, BCL2, BCL6 rearrangements), and flow cytometry to confirm Richter transformation and subtype", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Hold zanubrutinib immediately given suspected Richter transformation and disease progression on BTK inhibitor", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Broad-spectrum IV antibiotics (e.g., piperacillin-tazobactam) empirically given immunosuppression, painful indurated gluteal mass overlying old animal-bite scars, and elevated D-dimer suggesting possible concurrent infectious process", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Once biopsy confirms DLBCL-type Richter transformation, initiate R-EPOCH (rituximab, etoposide, prednisone, vincristine, cyclophosphamide, doxorubicin) or R-CHOP chemoimmunotherapy with dose adjustment for age and performance status", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Therapeutic anticoagulation evaluation: D-dimer 2987 \u00b5g/L with RV dilatation and pulmonary hypertension on echo raises concern for VTE/PE; obtain lower-extremity Doppler and consider LMWH if confirmed or high clinical suspicion", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "PET-CT scan (full staging) once available to map disease burden, identify the most metabolically active lesion for biopsy guidance, and establish baseline for response assessment", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Supportive care: IV albumin infusion for hypoalbuminemia (2.8 g/dL), diuresis for ascites and bilateral pleural effusions (furosemide with electrolyte monitoring), red cell transfusion support for Hb 9.2 g/dL, and IVIG 0.4 g/kg for severe hypogammaglobulinemia (IgG 359 mg/dL) to reduce infection risk", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Allopurinol continuation and IV hydration with close uric acid/creatinine/electrolyte monitoring for tumor lysis syndrome prophylaxis prior to and during chemotherapy initiation", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 3}, {"action": "Multidisciplinary review (hematology, infectious disease, cardiology) given complex comorbidities, cardiac findings (pulmonary hypertension, RV dilatation), and immunosuppressed state prior to chemotherapy", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 3}]}}}

solve.working_diagnosis: Richter Transformation of CLL to Diffuse Large B-Cell Lymphoma (DLBCL), with bulky nodal disease, hepatosplenic involvement, ascites, pleural effusions, and extranodal gluteal mass (at prior animal-bite scar site), on BTK inhibitor background
differentials: De novo DLBCL with concurrent CLL
Soft tissue infection / necrotizing fasciitis at old animal-bite scar site (immunocompromised host on zanubrutinib)
Primary effusion lymphoma or plasmablastic lymphoma

treatment_plan.action: Urgent core biopsy of the gluteal mass (and/or a readily accessible enlarged node) for histopathology, immunohistochemistry (CD20, CD10, BCL6, MYC, BCL2, Ki-67), FISH (MYC, BCL2, BCL6 rearrangements), and flow cytometry to confirm Richter transformation and subtype
Hold zanubrutinib immediately given suspected Richter transformation and disease progression on BTK inhibitor

Broad-spectrum IV antibiotics (e.g., piperacillin-tazobactam) empirically given immunosuppression, painful indurated gluteal mass overlying old animal-bite scars, and elevated D-dimer suggesting possible concurrent infectious process

Once biopsy confirms DLBCL-type Richter transformation, initiate R-EPOCH (rituximab, etoposide, prednisone, vincristine, cyclophosphamide, doxorubicin) or R-CHOP chemoimmunotherapy with dose adjustment for age and performance status

Therapeutic anticoagulation evaluation: D-dimer 2987 µg/L with RV dilatation and pulmonary hypertension on echo raises concern for VTE/PE; obtain lower-extremity Doppler and consider LMWH if confirmed or high clinical suspicion
PET-CT scan (full staging) once available to map disease burden, identify the most metabolically active lesion for biopsy guidance, and establish baseline for response assessment

Supportive care: IV albumin infusion for hypoalbuminemia (2.8 g/dL), diuresis for ascites and bilateral pleural effusions (furosemide with electrolyte monitoring), red cell transfusion support for Hb 9.2 g/dL, and IVIG 0.4 g/kg for severe hypogammaglobulinemia (IgG 359 mg/dL) to reduce infection risk
Allopurinol continuation and IV hydration with close uric acid/creatinine/electrolyte monitoring for tumor lysis syndrome prophylaxis prior to and during chemotherapy initiation

Multidisciplinary review (hematology, infectious disease, cardiology) given complex comorbidities, cardiac findings (pulmonary hypertension, RV dilatation), and immunosuppressed state prior to chemotherapy

content: {"evaluation": {"working_diagnosis": "correct", "differentials": ["correct", "incorrect", "correct", "incorrect", "incorrect"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "correct", "intention": "correct", "priority": "correct"}, {"action": "incorrect", "intention": "incorrect", "priority": "incorrect"}, {"action": "incorrect", "intention": "incorrect", "priority": "incorrect"}, {"action": "incorrect", "intention": "incorrect", "priority": "incorrect"}, {"action": "partially_correct", "intention": "correct", "priority": "partially_correct"}, {"action": "correct", "intention": "correct", "priority": "partially_correct"}, {"action": "correct", "intention": "correct", "priority": "correct"}, {"action": "incorrect", "intention": "incorrect", "priority": "incorrect"}, {"action": "incorrect", "intention": "incorrect", "priority": "incorrect"}]}}

Round 2 begins.
Setting: Inpatient diagnostic unit
Capabilities: The patient can undergo targeted imaging, bone marrow or tissue sampling, and comprehensive flow cytometry while longer-turnaround molecular studies are still pending.
Request additional information or solve.

content: {"response": {"action": "request", "request": "core biopsy of the gluteal mass with histopathology, immunohistochemistry, and flow cytometry"}}

response.request: core biopsy of the gluteal mass with histopathology, immunohistochemistry, and flow cytometry

content:
Extended Evaluations:
CT thorax (2025-09-26): segmental pulmonary embolism right lower lobe with infarction/infarct pneumonia; bilateral pleural effusions. PET-CT (2025-09-30): intensely FDG-avid nodal disease (cervical to pelvic), bulky retroperitoneal/hepatic nodes, avid gluteal mass, uptake in infarct zone. Gluteal biopsy (10-01): diffuse large B-cell lymphoma CD19⁺/CD20⁺/PAX5⁺/CD5⁺/CD23⁻, Ki-67 ~80%. Lumbar puncture: clear CSF, no malignant cells.

{"response": {"action": "solve", "solve": {"working_diagnosis": "Richter Transformation to DLBCL (CD19+/CD20+/PAX5+/CD5+, Ki-67 ~80%), stage IV bulky disease (cervical to pelvic FDG-avid nodes, hepatic hilum, extranodal gluteal mass), complicated by confirmed segmental pulmonary embolism with right lower lobe infarct pneumonia; no CNS involvement on LP", "differentials": ["CLL accelerated progression without histologic transformation", "De novo CD5+ DLBCL with incidental CLL"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "Initiate therapeutic anticoagulation with low-molecular-weight heparin (e.g., enoxaparin weight-based dosing adjusted for renal function and Plt 78 \u00d710\u2079/L) for confirmed segmental pulmonary embolism with right lower lobe infarction", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Start R-CHOP (rituximab 375 mg/m\u00b2, cyclophosphamide, doxorubicin, vincristine, prednisone) chemoimmunotherapy for confirmed DLBCL-type Richter transformation, with dose modifications for age 77, reduced LVEF borderline, hypoalbuminemia, and thrombocytopenia", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Administer IV antibiotics (e.g., piperacillin-tazobactam) for right lower lobe infarct pneumonia in an immunocompromised host prior to and concurrent with chemotherapy initiation", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Intrathecal methotrexate CNS prophylaxis (4\u20136 doses) given high-risk Richter DLBCL features: elevated LDH 852 U/L, Ki-67 ~80%, extranodal involvement, and CD5+ phenotype, despite negative LP", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "IVIG replacement (0.4 g/kg IV) for severe hypogammaglobulinemia (IgG 359 mg/dL) to reduce risk of serious infection during immunochemotherapy", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Transfuse packed red blood cells to maintain Hb \u22658\u20139 g/dL (current Hb 9.2 g/dL) and platelet support as needed to facilitate safe anticoagulation and chemotherapy delivery", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Refer for allogeneic stem cell transplantation assessment in first complete remission, given Richter transformation on BTK inhibitor with high-risk biology (CD5+, Ki-67 80%), if performance status permits", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 3}]}}}

solve.working_diagnosis: Richter Transformation to DLBCL (CD19+/CD20+/PAX5+/CD5+, Ki-67 ~80%), stage IV bulky disease (cervical to pelvic FDG-avid nodes, hepatic hilum, extranodal gluteal mass), complicated by confirmed segmental pulmonary embolism with right lower lobe infarct pneumonia; no CNS involvement on LP
differentials: CLL accelerated progression without histologic transformation
De novo CD5+ DLBCL with incidental CLL

treatment_plan.action: Initiate therapeutic anticoagulation with low-molecular-weight heparin (e.g., enoxaparin weight-based dosing adjusted for renal function and Plt 78 ×10⁹/L) for confirmed segmental pulmonary embolism with right lower lobe infarction

Start R-CHOP (rituximab 375 mg/m², cyclophosphamide, doxorubicin, vincristine, prednisone) chemoimmunotherapy for confirmed DLBCL-type Richter transformation, with dose modifications for age 77, reduced LVEF borderline, hypoalbuminemia, and thrombocytopenia
Administer IV antibiotics (e.g., piperacillin-tazobactam) for right lower lobe infarct pneumonia in an immunocompromised host prior to and concurrent with chemotherapy initiation
Intrathecal methotrexate CNS prophylaxis (4–6 doses) given high-risk Richter DLBCL features: elevated LDH 852 U/L, Ki-67 ~80%, extranodal involvement, and CD5+ phenotype, despite negative LP
IVIG replacement (0.4 g/kg IV) for severe hypogammaglobulinemia (IgG 359 mg/dL) to reduce risk of serious infection during immunochemotherapy
Transfuse packed red blood cells to maintain Hb ≥8–9 g/dL (current Hb 9.2 g/dL) and platelet support as needed to facilitate safe anticoagulation and chemotherapy delivery

Refer for allogeneic stem cell transplantation assessment in first complete remission, given Richter transformation on BTK inhibitor with high-risk biology (CD5+, Ki-67 80%), if performance status permits

Round 3 begins.
Setting: Definitive staging and planning phase
Capabilities: Full cytogenetic, molecular, and response assessments plus historical treatment data are accessible to guide transplant or cellular therapy planning.
Request additional information or solve.
Final round - provide definitive diagnosis and treatment plan.

content: {"response": {"action": "request", "request": "FISH panel for MYC, BCL2, BCL6 rearrangements and TP53 deletion/mutation; IGHV mutation status and CLL cytogenetics"}}

response.request: FISH panel for MYC, BCL2, BCL6 rearrangements and TP53 deletion/mutation; IGHV mutation status and CLL cytogenetics

content:
Cytogenetics:
Karyotype: 46,XY,del(11)(q22q23),+12,del(13)(q14q21),del(17)(p13)[17]/46,XY[4]; FISH confirms 17p deletion and trisomy 12.

{"response": {"action": "solve", "solve": {"working_diagnosis": "Richter Transformation to DLBCL (CD5+, Ki-67 ~80%), stage IV bulky disease with confirmed del(17p13)/TP53 deletion, del(11q)/ATM, trisomy 12, and del(13q) by complex karyotype \u2014 highest-risk Richter biology, progressing on zanubrutinib, complicated by segmental PE with right lower lobe infarct pneumonia and no CNS involvement", "differentials": ["De novo CD5+ DLBCL with underlying CLL (clonally related vs. unrelated \u2014 distinguishable by IGHV/FISH clonality studies)", "CLL acceleration without full histologic transformation (excluded by biopsy Ki-67 ~80% and diffuse large B-cell morphology)"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "Continue therapeutic anticoagulation with LMWH (enoxaparin, dose-adjusted for creatinine 1.4 mg/dL and platelets 78\u00d710\u2079/L) for confirmed segmental pulmonary embolism with right lower lobe infarction; transition to direct oral anticoagulant (e.g., rivaroxaban) once platelet count and renal function stabilize", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Initiate DA-R-EPOCH (dose-adjusted rituximab, etoposide, prednisone, vincristine, cyclophosphamide, doxorubicin) as preferred regimen over R-CHOP for del(17p)-positive, Ki-67 ~80%, high-risk Richter DLBCL with bulky stage IV disease; adjust dosing for age 77, borderline LVEF 51%, hypoalbuminemia, and thrombocytopenia", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Treat right lower lobe infarct pneumonia with IV piperacillin-tazobactam (adjusted for renal function) until clinical and radiologic resolution prior to or concurrent with first chemotherapy cycle", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Expedite referral for allogeneic stem cell transplantation (reduced-intensity conditioning given age 77) as consolidation in first complete remission, given TP53-deleted Richter transformation arising on BTK inhibitor \u2014 this represents the only potentially curative strategy", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Enroll in clinical trial for TP53-deleted/BTK inhibitor\u2013refractory Richter transformation; options include venetoclax + obinutuzumab, polatuzumab vedotin-based combinations, or bispecific antibodies (e.g., epcoritamab, glofitamab) if chemoimmunotherapy fails or as frontline in trial setting", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Implement comprehensive infection prophylaxis during DA-R-EPOCH: continue aciclovir for HSV/VZV prophylaxis, add TMP-SMX (or inhaled pentamidine if sulfa-intolerant) for PCP prophylaxis, and consider antifungal prophylaxis (fluconazole) given profound hypogammaglobulinemia (IgG 359 mg/dL) and anticipated prolonged neutropenia", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 3}]}}}

solve.working_diagnosis: Richter Transformation to DLBCL (CD5+, Ki-67 ~80%), stage IV bulky disease with confirmed del(17p13)/TP53 deletion, del(11q)/ATM, trisomy 12, and del(13q) by complex karyotype — highest-risk Richter biology, progressing on zanubrutinib, complicated by segmental PE with right lower lobe infarct pneumonia and no CNS involvement
differentials: De novo CD5+ DLBCL with underlying CLL (clonally related vs. unrelated — distinguishable by IGHV/FISH clonality studies)
CLL acceleration without full histologic transformation (excluded by biopsy Ki-67 ~80% and diffuse large B-cell morphology)

treatment_plan.action: Continue therapeutic anticoagulation with LMWH (enoxaparin, dose-adjusted for creatinine 1.4 mg/dL and platelets 78×10⁹/L) for confirmed segmental pulmonary embolism with right lower lobe infarction; transition to direct oral anticoagulant (e.g., rivaroxaban) once platelet count and renal function stabilize

Initiate DA-R-EPOCH (dose-adjusted rituximab, etoposide, prednisone, vincristine, cyclophosphamide, doxorubicin) as preferred regimen over R-CHOP for del(17p)-positive, Ki-67 ~80%, high-risk Richter DLBCL with bulky stage IV disease; adjust dosing for age 77, borderline LVEF 51%, hypoalbuminemia, and thrombocytopenia
Treat right lower lobe infarct pneumonia with IV piperacillin-tazobactam (adjusted for renal function) until clinical and radiologic resolution prior to or concurrent with first chemotherapy cycle

Expedite referral for allogeneic stem cell transplantation (reduced-intensity conditioning given age 77) as consolidation in first complete remission, given TP53-deleted Richter transformation arising on BTK inhibitor — this represents the only potentially curative strategy

Enroll in clinical trial for TP53-deleted/BTK inhibitor–refractory Richter transformation; options include venetoclax + obinutuzumab, polatuzumab vedotin-based combinations, or bispecific antibodies (e.g., epcoritamab, glofitamab) if chemoimmunotherapy fails or as frontline in trial setting

Implement comprehensive infection prophylaxis during DA-R-EPOCH: continue aciclovir for HSV/VZV prophylaxis, add TMP-SMX (or inhaled pentamidine if sulfa-intolerant) for PCP prophylaxis, and consider antifungal prophylaxis (fluconazole) given profound hypogammaglobulinemia (IgG 359 mg/dL) and anticipated prolonged neutropenia
